title: carya pallida var. pyriformis
intro: Carya pallida var. pyriformis, also known as the pyriform hickory or shagbark hickory, is a variety of hickory tree native to North America, characterized by its distinctive shaggy bark and edible nuts. The medicinal properties of Carya pallida var. pyriformis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural antiseptic. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of allergies and colds. Carya pallida var. pyriformis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its nuts can be eaten raw or roasted for their nutty flavor and nutritional benefits.
